column3: AI-powered CT scan analysis for automated brain haemorrhage detection and classification using MedNext models
column13: Research project at La Trobe University developing AI models to categorize brain regions via CT scans and detect haemorrhage types. Implemented baseline MedNext models with co-learning training modifications for improved accuracy.
column14: Medical imaging requires high precision and interpretability. Balancing model complexity with clinical deployment needs.
column15: Adapted MedNext architecture with co-learning training methods to enhance segmentation accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency for clinical settings.
column16: Successfully established baseline models for brain haemorrhage classification, contributing to ongoing medical AI research.

column2: Anomaly Detection with LSTM in Clinical Lab
column3: Honours thesis implementing Patient Based Real-Time Quality Control using LSTM neural networks for early anomaly detection
column6: Python, LSTM, Neural Networks, Statistical Methods
column13: Honours thesis project implementing Patient Based Real-Time Quality Control (PBRTQC) system using statistical methods and LSTM neural networks. Addresses limitations of traditional quality control that catches errors only after results reach patients.
column14: Traditional QC methods are costly and reactive. Need for real-time anomaly detection before results reach patients.
column15: Developed LSTM-based unsupervised anomaly detection system for clinical laboratory data. Combined statistical methods with deep learning for early error detection.
column16: Evaluated effectiveness of LSTM techniques for clinical anomaly detection, providing foundation for proactive quality control systems.

column3: Convolutional model outperforming transformers for long-tailed bone density prediction from CT scans
column6: Python, CNN, Bal-CE Loss, CT Imaging
column13: Published research introducing MedConv, a convolutional approach for bone density prediction via CT scans. Addresses computational complexity of transformers and long-tailed data distribution in clinical settings. Achieved 21% accuracy improvement over SOTA methods.
column14: High computational complexity of transformer architectures limiting portable deployment. Imbalanced, long-tailed hospital data skewing predictions.
column15: Developed MedConv using pure convolutions with Bal-CE loss and post-hoc logit adjustment. Optimized for clinical deployment with lower computational demands.
column16: Published on arXiv with 21% accuracy and 20% ROC AUC improvement. Validated on AustinSpine dataset for clinical applicability.
